subject: Anthropology
title: Digital Commons
description: A micro-lesson that makes Digital Commons usable.

hook.text: Wikipedia, open-source code, and Creative Commons. The internet has revived the idea of shared knowledge resources.

buildup.text: Digital commons are shared online resources — software, data, and knowledge — that anyone can access and contribute to.

discovery.text: Open-source projects like Linux prove that collective production can rival corporate products in quality and scale.

twist.text: Tech companies profit from the digital commons while enclosing them. Free data gets harvested and sold back to users.

climax.text: The digital commons are contested ground. The same internet that enables sharing also enables new forms of enclosure.

punchline.text: The battle between sharing and owning continues online.

quiz.question: What are digital commons?
options: Shared online resources anyone can access and contribute to
